Question (example wording — replace with: Do you have a documented incident response plan that is tested?
Your answer: Yes — documented IR plan with severity definitions and escalation paths; last tabletop exercise ran in March 2026.

Question (example wording — replace with: Do you maintain a risk register reviewed by leadership?
Is there a named owner for information security risk?
Do you maintain a written information security policy approved by management?
How often are security policies reviewed and re-approved?
Do employees receive security awareness training at hire and periodically after?
Are background checks performed where legally permitted?
Do you maintain an inventory of systems that store or process customer data?
Is customer data classified and handled according to a data classification standard?
Is multi-factor authentication enforced for remote and administrative access?
Are privileged accounts separated from day-to-day user accounts?
Is access revoked within a defined time after termination?
Is code reviewed before release to production?
Do you run security testing (SAST/DAST or penetration tests) on a defined schedule?
How are secrets and credentials managed in your build and runtime environments?
Is network access segmented between production and corporate environments?
Are firewalls or security groups reviewed on a schedule?
Are systems hardened to a documented baseline and patched on a defined cadence?
Are laptops encrypted and centrally managed?
Which cloud providers and regions host customer data?
Is customer data encrypted at rest and in transit? Which algorithms/protocols?
Do you maintain audit logs for production systems and review them?
Is there a documented change management process for production changes?
Will you notify customers of a security incident within a defined timeframe?
Do you have documented business continuity and disaster recovery plans?
What are your recovery time (RTO) and recovery point (RPO) objectives?
Do you perform vulnerability scanning and track remediation to closure?
Do you have a privacy policy covering collection use and retention of personal data?
Do you support data subject deletion/export requests within defined timelines?
Do you assess the security of your own subprocessors and vendors?
Will you provide your subprocessor list and notify customers of changes?
